users.title: Users
users.description: List of user identifiers (emails, IPs, or UUIDs as strings) to add or remove from the segment. Maximum 10,000 users per request. Required if media_id is not provided.

media_id.title: Media Id
media_id.description: UUID of the uploaded media file containing the membership list. Required if users is not provided.

remove.title: Remove
remove.description: If True, removes users from the segment. If False (default), adds users to the segment.

entity_level.description: Entity level for the report: 'campaign', 'adset', or 'ad'

adset_ids.title: Adset Ids
adset_ids.description: Optional list of ad set IDs

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: Optional dimensions to group by. Allowed values: device_type, dma, state, zip_code

attribution_window.description: Attribution window for conversion metrics (7_day, 14_day, 30_day). Defaults to 7_day if not provided.

ScheduledReportBody.title: ScheduledReportBody
ScheduledReportBody.description:
Request body for scheduling a report - simplified interface.

This simplified interface automatically handles many complexities similar to the AdsManager UI:

1. **Entity Level**: The `entity_level` field determines:
   - Which default fields are included in the report (campaign, adset, or ad-specific fields)
   - Which entity ID dimension is automatically added (campaign_id, adset_id, or ad_id)

2. **Automatic Filter Generation**: Filters are automatically created from:
   - `start_date` and `end_date` (date range filters)
   - `adaccount_ids` (required filter)
   - `campaign_ids`, `adset_ids`, `ad_ids` (optional entity filters)

3. **Default Fields**: A predefined set of fields is automatically included based on `entity_level`:
   - Campaign level: campaign-specific fields (campaign_id, campaign_name, campaign_status, etc.)
   - Adset level: adset-specific fields (adset_id, adset_name, adset_status, etc.)
   - Ad level: ad-specific fields (ad_id, ad_name, ad_status, etc.)
   All levels include common performance metrics (spend, impressions, cpm, conversions, etc.)

4. **Dimensions**:
   - Dimensions serve dual purpose: they group the data AND are automatically added as fields
   - The entity-level dimension (campaign_id/adset_id/ad_id) is always included based on `entity_level`
   - Additional dimensions from the `dimensions` list are added both as grouping dimensions and as output fields

5. **Output Format**: Always CSV (not configurable)

Example:
    {
        "entity_level": "campaign",
        "start_date": "2025-01-01T00:00:00",
        "end_date": "2025-01-31T23:59:59",
        "adaccount_ids": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2"],
        "campaign_ids": ["campaign-uuid"],
        "dimensions": ["state", "dma"],
        "time_aggregation": "day",
        "limit": 50000
    }
